articles.title: A Sad Story About People Trying to Cross the Sea
articles.summary: Four people died in the sea near France while trying to get on a boat to go to the UK. They were swept away by strong currents. Thirty-eight other people were rescued, and two children went to the hospital to be checked. The boat kept going with about 30 people on it. The UK government said they are very sad and want to stop these dangerous trips. People try to cross the sea because they want a better life, but it is very risky. The weather was calm, which made more people try to go. Some groups say we need safer ways for people to travel so they don't have to take such dangerous journeys.

articles.title: Dogs Saved from a Crowded House Are Happy Now
articles.summary: The RSPCA, an animal charity, found over 250 poodle-cross dogs in a very crowded living room in the UK earlier this year. The dogs were rescued because their living conditions had become too much to handle. Lee Hopgood from the RSPCA said the dogs are doing really well now. Many dogs had matted and crusted coats that needed treatment. After being cared for, many dogs have been rehomed to forever homes by Dogs Trust, another charity. Some dogs are still with Dogs Trust, including puppies born there. One dog named Boone was adopted by Dermot Murphy. Boone was underweight and had sore ears and eyes at first, but now he can play with a ball and is part of the family. The RSPCA says such cases might happen due to mental health issues, money problems, or bad breeding.

articles.title: A Famous Author Shares Her Real Name
articles.summary: Freida McFadden is a very popular author who writes exciting books like The Housemaid. She has always used a fake name, but now she has told everyone her real name is Sara Cohen. She did this because she was tired of keeping it a secret and wanted people to know she is a real person. Freida McFadden is a pen name she picked as a joke from a medical database. She is also a doctor who helps people with brain problems in Boston. She wrote her first book in 2013 and has sold millions of copies, making her one of the best-selling authors in the UK. She kept her writing and doctor jobs separate so her patients would not feel strange. Now, she is happy to share her true identity with her readers.

articles.title: Students Asked to Return Money for Weekend Classes
articles.summary: Over 20,000 students who take weekend classes have been told they got money they shouldn't have. This money is called maintenance loans and grants, which help pay for things like food and housing while studying. The Student Loans Company sent letters saying the students' courses were not allowed to get this money because they only meet on weekends. Now, the students must pay back the money. Some students also got extra help for childcare, which they might not have to repay. The universities say it's not their fault, but the government says there was a mistake or someone broke the rules. Students are upset and can ask for help if paying back is hard.

articles.title: Will the Peace in Iran Last?
articles.summary: This article talks about a ceasefire in Iran, which is an agreement to stop fighting. Two former U.S. officials, Barbara Leaf and Michael Doran, share their thoughts. Barbara was a diplomat who worked in the Middle East, and Michael was a security expert. They are worried that the ceasefire is fragile, meaning it might not hold for long. They spoke to officials from Gulf countries who also think it's shaky. The ceasefire gives time for peace, but there are problems, like disagreements about Lebanon being part of the deal. This makes it volatile, which means things could change quickly. The article helps us understand why peace is hard to keep in this region.

articles.title: NATO Leader Visits President to Help Friends Get Along
articles.summary: A leader from NATO, a group of countries that work together for safety, met with President Trump at the White House. They talked to try to stop arguments between the U.S. and NATO. The president had said NATO did not help enough in a war. Also, Southwest Airlines is making people pay more money to put bags on planes because fuel costs went up from the war. Other airlines like Delta are doing this too, but some people like soldiers still get free bags. In Georgia, a new person named Clay Fuller will join Congress after winning an election. He supports President Trump and will work in Washington, D.C. Weather experts say last March was very warm, and stock prices went up because people hope for peace in the war.
